title: astragalus oxyphysopsis
intro: Astragalus oxyphysopsis, also known as White Milk Vetch or Locoweed, is a perennial plant species in the legume family (Fabaceae) native to North America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Astragalus oxyphysopsis has been used to support immune function, reduce stress and fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Astragalus preparations are available in various forms, including capsules, teas, and extracts, which can be used to make herbal remedies, such as decoctions and infusions.
